title: Gemini/Imagen backend for image_gen: quality tier above FLUX-schnell at $0, reusing the existing GEMINI_API_KEY
source: Claude Fable audit 2026-07-02 (hackathon prep) + Ash request 2026-07-03 (asked for free, better image gen; Midjourney rejected - not free, no official API)
current_state: tools/tools_image.py has two backends: pollinations (default - free, keyless, HTTP GET) and huggingface (HF_TOKEN, FLUX.1-schnell, ~60 req/hr free tier). Gemini's image generation (Imagen via the Google AI Studio free tier) is a quality tier above FLUX-schnell for most prompts, and Pi already carries GEMINI_API_KEY for research mode and the router fallback chain (core/providers/gemini.py) - no new key, no new account.
target_state: A third backend 'gemini': _generate_gemini() following the exact _generate_pollinations/_generate_huggingface pattern (~40 lines) - request image, save bytes to the same output path convention, return the same result dict shape ({success, path, backend, ...}). ToolSpec schema enum gains 'gemini'. Default backend stays pollinations (keyless); gemini is opt-in per call.
migration_plan: 1. Check how core/providers/gemini.py talks to the API (SDK vs raw HTTP) and reuse the same transport style for the image endpoint - do not introduce a second Google client pattern.

2. Add _generate_gemini(prompt, ...) mirroring the existing backend functions: call the Imagen/Gemini image endpoint with GEMINI_API_KEY, decode returned image bytes, write to the standard output path, return the standard dict; missing key returns the same shaped error as the HF backend's missing-token case.
3. Add 'gemini' to the backend enum + one line in the tool description in the ToolSpec at the bottom of tools/tools_image.py.

4. Test: testing/test_image_gen_gemini.py - monkeypatch the HTTP/SDK call to return canned image bytes; assert file written and dict shape matches the other backends; missing-key path returns success:false with a helpful message. No real API calls (non-costly).
5. python scripts/verify.py PASS; run scripts/passive/consciousness_capability_sync.py if the tool description changed.
risk_notes: Free-tier Imagen has daily caps - fine for personal use; the error path for quota exhaustion should read like the HF rate-limit error, and the user can always fall back to pollinations. Do not make gemini the default: keyless-by-default is the right posture for image_gen.
